inputs.price_source: Insider Form 4 cluster purchases 2026-05-15 at $19.82; mechanical refresh will sync Yahoo RYN close 2026-06-10
inputs.fcf_source: Normalized Cash Available for Distribution (CAD): Q1 2026 Adjusted CAD $62.7M on ~256M diluted shares = $0.245/quarter; haircut ~40% for lumpy real estate rural/solar acreage sales; annualized ~$0.58/sh (10-Q 2026-05-08 Performance and Liquidity Indicators)
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: Stockholders equity $5,329M / 301.7M shares post-merger; timberlands carried at depletion basis not fair-value NAV
inputs.acres_source: 10-Q 2026-05-08: ~4.01M owned+leased acres (3.2M U.S. South, 930K U.S. Northwest) after PotlatchDeltic merger closed 2026-01-30
inputs.merger_note: Rayonier merged with PotlatchDeltic Corporation effective 2026-01-30; combined entity adds ~2.1M acres, six sawmills (Wood Products), and expanded Real Estate segment
inputs.large_disposition_fy2025_note: Standalone FY2025 included 55,000-acre large disposition ($291M operating income from large dispositions per 10-K FY2025); excluded from normalized CAD
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence base uses normalized CAD, not GAAP net income (Q1 2026 net loss $12.4M includes merger costs and tax items) or lumpy Real Estate segment EBITDA

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: GAAP equity ~$17.66/sh at 2026-03-31; timberlands at depletion basis ~$1,463/acre on books vs transaction comps often higher
optionality_gate.overlay_nav_note: [Assumption] timberland fair value ~$1,800/acre on 4.0M acres less ~$6.2/sh net debt; not GAAP book
optionality_gate.notes: Price ~12% above GAAP book post-merger; stance gate uses normalized CAD ten-year path, not book floor alone

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: 10-Q 2026-05-08: timber and timberlands at depletion basis; merger added PotlatchDeltic book values

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Per-acre fair marks on 4.1M acres vs Q1 2026 rural sale comps ($6,457/acre weighted; solar parcel $10,100/acre)
Merger synergy realization on Wood Products and overhead
Carbon capture and storage / solar lease option value on Southern acreage
Post-merger leverage path ($1.86B long-term debt; Q1 mandatory repayments $27.5M)

segments_or_options.label: Southern Timber (~3.16M acres)
segments_or_options.evidence: Q1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA $45.5M; carbon capture and solar contract revenue embedded

segments_or_options.label: Pacific Northwest Timber (~930K acres)
segments_or_options.evidence: Q1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA $8.6M; export log markets CFR basis

segments_or_options.label: Wood Products (six sawmills from PotlatchDeltic)
segments_or_options.evidence: Q1 2026 Adjusted EBITDA $6.8M; 288M board feet lumber shipments full quarter inclusive pre-merger

segments_or_options.label: Real Estate (HBU land sales, Chenal Valley)
segments_or_options.evidence: Q1 2026 7,695 acres sold ($59.8M); lumpy vs prior Q1 1,031 acres ($10.2M); improved development and rural solar options

segments_or_options.label: Carbon capture / solar lease optionality on Southern acreage
segments_or_options.evidence: 10-Q segment discussion: CCS and solar energy contract revenue in Southern Timber; Q1 rural solar sale $10,100/acre

segments.label: Wood Products
segments.evidence: Merger-added sawmill segment; cyclical lumber exposure

segments.label: Real Estate
segments.evidence: Q1 lumpy HBU sales; normalized run-rate well below Q1 $46.2M Adjusted EBITDA

options.label: Higher-and-better-use land bank
options.base_terminal_rationale: P_base 0.4 × ~$1.05B incremental HBU value above normalized timber run-rate
options.evidence: Q1 2026 rural/improved development sales; Chenal Valley residential lots

options.label: CCS / solar lease contracts
options.base_terminal_rationale: P_base 0.3 × contracted option value on Southern acreage
options.evidence: 10-Q Southern Timber CCS and solar revenue discussion

segment_build.corporate_drag_note: Interest on $1.86B debt, merger integration, corporate G&A not in segment EBITDA

bear.notes: Lumber downturn compresses Wood Products; Real Estate sales stall; leverage limits buybacks

base.notes: Normalized CAD $0.58/sh; merger scale benefits; modest HBU contribution

bull.notes: Synergy capture; CCS/solar contracts scale; lumber cycle recovery; multiple re-rate

human_review.notes: Merger closed 2026-01-30; first combined 10-Q is Q1 2026. Provisional normalized CAD excludes Q1 lumpy Real Estate. UPREIT structure (Rayonier, L.P.) with operating partnership units.

growth_explanation.base_mechanism: Merger scale on 4.1M acres; Southern timber harvest growth; Northwest export log premiums; selective HBU real estate crystallization
growth_explanation.bear_risk: Wood Products cyclicality; $1.86B debt stack; integration execution; lumber price downturn

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Timberlands at depletion basis $5.87B (~$1,463/acre); transaction comps often higher (Q1 rural $6,457/acre weighted)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage?
option_scan.evidence: 4.1M acres with HBU real estate and CCS/solar optionality not fully in run-rate CAD

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: All four reportable segments generated positive Q1 Adjusted EBITDA

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: CCS and solar energy contracts in Southern Timber; Real Estate development pipeline

option_scan.question: Private stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: No material private equity stakes post-merger

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution?
option_scan.evidence: Quarterly dividend $0.26/sh ($1.04 annual) covered by normalized CAD ~$0.58/sh

option_scan.question: Embedded in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Timber harvest and Wood Products revenue in operating segments

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

insider_signal.disclaimer: Context only. Insider activity informs scenario confidence and stance discussion; it does not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

scenario_confidence.note: Scenario weights are qualitative confidence only; Lawrence base IRR unchanged.

paths.label: Segment-derived growth (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

paths.label: NAV overlay dated payoff (7yr to overlay base)
paths.weight_why: Answers a different question (NAV convergence) than 7yr cash-flow IRR; capped weight to avoid theory conflation.
weight_falsifiers: NAV overlay base not updated when segment build or filings change >10%
floor_pass false but weight above 15% without [HUMAN REVIEW]

flags: NAV overlay is a different theory (asset payoff vs cash-flow IRR); weight capped at 10% to limit theory conflation.

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

capacity.note: Biological growth is the capacity clock.

pricing.note: Stumpage and lumber prices cycle with housing.

world_model_context.disclaimer: Context only. World Model fails and Superorg gaps flag [HUMAN REVIEW]; they do not auto-rewrite Lawrence base IRR. Promotion requires _system/reviews/pending/world_model_promote_{TICKER}_{date}.md.
